page: Amazon Web Services m5dn.xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro System. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, running at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The instance is configured with 4 vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It includes 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, translating to 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, a 150 GB NVMe SSD instance store is bundled. Network performance is defined by a baseline bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations place the m5dn.xlarge in the lower tiers for raw compute and memory throughput. Single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests, alongside Geekbench multi-core and Passmark CPU scores, rank in the bottom 25% of tested hardware. However, Geekbench single-core performance and Passmark memory scores reach average levels in the middle 50% bracket. Large-block memory read and write speeds are measured at 26.27 GB/sec and 23.05 GB/sec, respectively. Redis SET operations achieve 3.34 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput reaches 3.74 GB/sec, both falling into the bottom 25% performance category.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m5dn.xlarge balances general-purpose compute resources with dedicated local NVMe storage and enhanced networking capabilities. While raw CPU and memory bandwidth benchmarks are modest, the inclusion of local high-speed storage and EBS optimization makes it suitable for workloads requiring fast temporary storage and moderate network throughput. Appropriate workloads include general-purpose applications, small-scale databases, web serving, and development environments that benefit from local NVMe instance storage without requiring high-density compute power.
description: Amazon Web Services m5dn.xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor running at 3.1 GHz with 4 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance includes a bundled 150 GB NVMe SSD local instance store and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate average single-core Geekbench and Passmark memory performance, though multi-core compute, memory bandwidth, and database operations generally fall into the bottom 25%. Qualitatively, the instance offers a balanced resource profile for workloads requiring fast local temporary storage and moderate network throughput. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, small databases, and web serving.
